home_heading: I build useful websites and practice repos.
home_lead: I am Soeraj Balak, a beginner software developer building public projects like my portfolio website and my testing assignment repository. I work with HTML, CSS, JavaScript, TypeScript, PHP, Python, Symfony, and React.

home_current_work_item1: Building and maintaining public GitHub projects
home_current_work_item2: Improving backend skills with PHP and Symfony
home_current_work_item3: Creating dynamic user interfaces with React
home_current_work_item4: Exploring AI-assisted development and practical AI use cases

home_card_ai_desc: Exploring practical AI use cases and AI-assisted development
home_card_projects_desc: Creating full-stack applications and public repositories
home_card_services_desc: Offering web development and design services
home_project1_desc: A public multi-page portfolio repo with a secured admin dashboard, contact API, message management, and deployment-ready configuration.
home_project2_desc: A public practice repository used to test assignments, experiment with code changes, and keep my workflow sharp.
home_project3_desc: A set of responsive page and component builds used to improve layout systems, accessibility, and mobile-first design decisions.
footer_personal_text: "Small steps every day still move you forward." Current vibe: focused, calm, and building.
footer_metadata_text: Version v2.6 Built with HTML, CSS, JavaScript, Node.js, and PHP.
about_lead: I am Soeraj Balak, a beginner software developer focused on building practical, public-facing projects that improve my skills and showcase my progress. I work with HTML, CSS, JavaScript, TypeScript, PHP, Python, Symfony, and React, and I also use AI tools to support learning, development, and productivity.
about_approach_item1: I focus on writing clean, readable, and maintainable code.
about_approach_item2: I build responsive interfaces that work well on desktop and mobile.
about_approach_item3: I keep learning by building real public repositories and practical projects.
about_approach_item4: I combine frontend and backend basics to deliver practical full-stack solutions.
about_toolbox_item1: Frontend: HTML, CSS, JavaScript, TypeScript, React
about_toolbox_item3: Public repos: Portfolio Website Platform, Testing for an Assignment
about_toolbox_item4: AI: Using AI tools for learning, coding support, and productivity
about_toolbox_item5: Current goal: Build full-stack portfolio and business sites
projects_lead: A selection of the public GitHub repositories I am actively building and using to show frontend, backend, and full-stack development skills.
projects_repo1_desc: This public repository contains my multi-page portfolio with a secured admin dashboard, contact API, message management, and deployment-ready configuration.
projects_repo1_stack: Stack: HTML, CSS, JavaScript, PHP, Node.js
projects_repo2_desc: This public repository is my testing and assignment practice space where I try code changes, validate workflows, and keep my fundamentals sharp.
projects_repo2_focus: Focus: experimentation, validation, workflow practice, and small iterative improvements
projects_repo3_desc: A set of responsive page and component builds used to improve layout systems, accessibility, and mobile-first design decisions.
projects_repo3_focus: Focus: semantic HTML, reusable CSS patterns, and interactive JavaScript UI behavior
projects_code_lead: This keeps the page focused while still showing a small slice of the real code behind the site.
contact_lead: Use this form to send a message about my public repositories, a portfolio build, or another beginner-friendly web project. The backend API stores submissions in a local JSON file.
contact_runtime_non_json: API endpoint responded with non-JSON content.
contact_runtime_sent_notice: Thanks. Your message has been sent.
services_lead: I focus on small to medium projects where clean code, clear communication, and practical results matter, using the same approach I use in my public portfolio and testing repositories.
services_card1_desc: I build simple and responsive websites using HTML, CSS, and JavaScript, similar to the structure used in my public portfolio repository.
services_card2_desc: I create portfolio websites for students, starters, and freelancers who want a professional online profile like the one in my public portfolio repository.
services_card3_desc: I design and build clean landing pages for products, events, and small businesses.
services_card4_desc: I implement contact forms and basic backend functionality with PHP and structured data handling.
services_card5_desc: I build small React interfaces with reusable components, basic state management, and API integration.
services_card6_desc: I help with content updates, layout improvements, responsiveness issues, and basic bug fixes, the same kind of iteration I use in my testing repository.
updates_lead: A quick log of recent improvements across the portfolio.
updates_item1_desc: Added session-based admin auth flow, CSRF protection, optional OTP support, and inactivity timeout handling.
updates_item2_desc: Introduced migration SQL for runtime tables and a Node migration runner for PostgreSQL deployments.
updates_item3_desc: Language switching now updates key home/about content blocks instead of only the nav control.
updates_item4_desc: The projects section now links to dedicated breakdown pages with stack, highlights, and next steps for each repository.
updates_item5_desc: Robots and sitemap now use host-aware output with better crawl hints and freshness metadata.
updates_item6_desc: Desktop navigation now uses a hover reveal behavior focused on keeping the header minimal at rest.
updates_item7_desc: The bottom section now includes personal notes, build metadata, legal links, and this updates page.
updates_item8_desc: The temporary background music player and related assets were removed for a cleaner baseline.
updates_item9_desc: Added skip-link support, improved focus visibility, and hardened contact/admin request flows for better UX and stability.
privacy_lead: This page explains what this portfolio stores and what it does not store.
privacy_item1_desc: When you send a message through the contact form, submitted details may be stored for review and response.
privacy_item2_desc: This site does not currently run third-party analytics or ad tracking scripts.
privacy_item3_desc: If privacy handling changes, this page will be updated with a short note and date.
error404_lead: This link does not exist or may have moved.
error500_lead: Something went wrong on our side. Please try again in a moment.
my_status_heading: Building, refining, and keeping things simple.
my_hero_lead: This page is a lighter, more personal corner of the portfolio. It is for quick notes, links, and small things that do not fit the main about page.
my_rightnow_item1: Keeping a simple page that feels more personal than the about section
my_rightnow_item2: Collecting links, notes, and small updates in one place
my_rightnow_item3: Leaving room for experiments, interests, and casual updates
my_rightnow_item4: Keeping the design clean and easy to scan
my_card1_desc: Calm, focused, and usually tweaking something small. I like having a quiet page that feels like a personal corner instead of a formal profile.
my_card2_item1: VS Code for building and editing quickly
my_card2_item2: HTML and CSS when I want full control over the page
my_card2_item3: JavaScript for small interactions and utility behavior
my_card2_item4: PHP and Node when the page needs backend support
my_card3_item1: Cleaning up little parts of this portfolio
my_card3_item2: Thinking through what to keep simple and what to expand
my_card3_item3: Building pages that feel more personal and less formal
my_card3_item4: Trying small ideas before turning them into bigger changes
my_notes_item1: I like keeping a personal space separate from the main portfolio pages.
my_notes_item2: I prefer pages that are easy to skim and not overloaded with text.
my_notes_item3: I like having a place for links, notes, and small updates.
my_notes_item4: I usually end up refining details after the main idea is already there.
my_ideas_heading: What this page can show next
my_ideas_item1: A short check-in about what I'm working on
my_ideas_item2: A rotating list of favorite tools or sites
my_ideas_item3: Small wins, updates, or current interests
my_ideas_item4: Anything that feels more like a personal page than a profile
my_hobby1_desc: I listen to a bit of everything depending on my mood, but I usually come back to hip-hop, rap, and breakcore the most.
my_hobby2_desc: I am really into manga and manhwa (Korean manga), and I like following long stories, character growth, and good world-building. I also watch a lot of anime in my free time.
my_hobby3_desc: I am into strategy games, especially series like Total War and Crusader Kings, because I enjoy planning, managing resources, and building long campaigns that evolve over time.
my_links_lead: A few places where I keep my public presence and a way to reach me.
p1_lead: This project combines a static multi-page frontend with an Express backend, admin authentication, message handling, and deployment-oriented configuration.
p1_challenge_desc: Building a portfolio that demonstrates full-stack capabilities while remaining secure, maintainable, and showcasing best practices in modern web development.
p1_challenge_item1: Needed multi-layer security (authentication, rate limiting, XSS prevention)
p1_challenge_item2: Required message persistence and admin management
p1_challenge_item3: Had to work across multiple runtime environments
p1_solution_desc: A polyglot architecture combining static content delivery with protected API endpoints, layered security middleware, and comprehensive monitoring.
p1_solution_item1: Express server with Helmet security headers
p1_solution_item2: Session-based admin authentication with OTP support
p1_solution_item3: Per-endpoint rate limiting and input sanitization
p1_solution_item4: Sentry error tracking for production monitoring
p1_results_item3: Admin dashboard with analytics and filtering
p1_arch_lead: The platform uses a layered approach with clear separation of concerns.
p1_journey_lead: Building this portfolio revealed key insights about web development practices.
p1_deploy_lead: The platform is configured for multiple deployment environments.
p2_lead: This repository is used to practice assignment-style implementations, workflow checks, and repeatable verification steps before applying them to larger projects.
p2_purpose_desc: Provides a low-risk environment to experiment with code patterns, test deployment strategies, and validate new approaches before applying them to production repositories.
p2_practice_desc: Uses a disciplined approach to changes with validation at each step, ensuring that any adopted pattern is production-ready before wider application.
p2_results_desc: This disciplined approach has resulted in cleaner production repositories, fewer regressions, and more confident deployments across all projects.
p2_key_lead: The testing repository enforces a set of practices that have proven effective across multiple projects.
p2_integration_lead: Successful patterns from this repository are carefully selected and integrated into main projects with documentation.
p2_promotion_lead: Patterns are promoted to production projects when they meet these standards:
admin_lead: Enter your admin credentials to load message submissions.
admin_search_placeholder: Find by name, email, subject, message
admin_delivery_php: Email delivery mode: PHP mail() fallback
admin_delivery_disabled: Email delivery mode: Disabled (CONTACT_NOTIFY_TO not set)
admin_non_json: API endpoint responded with non-JSON content.
admin_session_timeout: Session timed out due to inactivity.
